---
title: "P00 | Loop HTML Report"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(rmdreportdeck)

samples <- data.frame(
  sample = paste0("S", 1:4),
  value = c(2, 5, 3, 6),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
```

# Description

This example shows the loop-aware panel helpers for repeated outputs such as one figure per sample or pathway.

## Per-sample panels

```{r results='asis', echo=FALSE}
items <- vector("list", nrow(samples))

for (i in seq_len(nrow(samples))) {
  row <- samples[i, , drop = FALSE]
  plot_obj <- ggplot(row, aes(sample, value, fill = sample)) +
    geom_col(show.legend = FALSE)
  bundle <- report_plot_bundle(
    plot_obj,
    stem = paste0("sample-", row$sample),
    width = 5,
    height = 3.5,
    data = row
  )

  items[[i]] <- report_item_panel(
    title = paste0("Sample ", row$sample, ": synthetic profile"),
    asset_bar = report_bundle_asset_bar(
      "Downloads",
      bundle,
      tsv_label = "TSV"
    ),
    note = "Each item is a nested collapsible panel produced inside a loop.",
    report_item_plot(plot_obj, width = 5, height = 3.5),
    report_item_table(row)
  )
}

report_loop_section(items)
```
